title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC made their NWSL debut at Gillette Stadium against Gotham FC, drawing over 30,000 fans.
Gotham FC defeated Boston Legacy FC 1-0 in a match marked by physical play and a record-tying eight yellow cards.
Both coaches embraced the rivalry's intensity, with Legacy FC's Patão focusing on improving decision-making and ball possession.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway on March 13, kicking off St. Patrick's Day celebrations.
The band performed a politically charged set, including 'Citizen I.C.E.', a protest against ICE, during their energetic show.
Openers for the event included Boston bands The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown, setting the night's tone.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes in 2008, Bennett supports Joslin Diabetes Center for their role in managing his condition.
Bennett aims to raise awareness and funds for Type 1 diabetes and inspire others with similar challenges.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination, becoming the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
The Springfield native was the first Black person to win an Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019.
Carter's nomination is for her work on 'Sinners,' and she might make history with a third Oscar win.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools warn of over 200 layoffs if a property-tax override fails to pass at the May election.
The school district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staffing and programs, officials stated.
Officials cited financial strain from rising costs and limited revenue growth due to Proposition 2½ capping property-tax increases.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops to reduce puppy mill sales.
The bill proposes partnerships between pet stores and animal shelters for adoptions, promoting responsible pet ownership.
The legislation seeks to address housing and insurance barriers to make pet ownership more accessible in Massachusetts.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts officials urge water conservation despite heavy snowfall and persistent drought conditions across the state.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts are classified as Level 3 Critical Drought, second-highest in the state’s drought management system.
Residents are advised to monitor water use and make repairs to minimize waste amid ongoing water scarcity.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph explores why some women are hiding boyfriends online to distance themselves from traditional heterosexual models.
Joseph's essay on changes in women's online relationship dynamics sparked widespread debate and artistic tributes.
The discussion highlights questions about relationship centrality and independent identity in modern heterosexual partnerships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O’Neill, 27, is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp for children with serious medical conditions.
O’Neill has a personal connection to the cause; his fiancée’s sister, a cancer survivor, was once a camper and is now a counselor.
The camp provides a medically safe environment for kids to experience fun and friendship, offering services free of charge.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles have decided not to trade wide receiver A.J. Brown to the Patriots at this time.
Trade discussions may resume closer to June 1 due to potential salary cap benefits for the Eagles.
The Patriots reportedly offered first- and third-round draft picks for Brown, but the offer was denied.
